title: Spatial-TTT: Streaming Visual-based Spatial Intelligence with Test-Time Training
task: Streaming spatial understanding from long-horizon videos
inputs: Unbounded video streams
outputs: 3D spatial scene understanding and segmentation
key_results: State-of-the-art on video spatial benchmarks; handles long-horizon scene videos with efficient large-chunk updates
comments: Test-time training of fast weights is novel for streaming contexts, though computational overhead during inference may limit deployment.

title: Strategic Navigation or Stochastic Search? How Agents and Humans Reason Over Document Collections
task: Assessing agentic reasoning over document collections
inputs: User questions and PDF documents
outputs: Retrieved documents and answers with accuracy/effort tradeoff metrics
key_results: Best agents match human accuracy but rely on brute-force search; ~20% gap to oracle performance persists
comments: MADQA benchmark reveals agents lack genuine strategic planning despite high raw accuracy, pointing to fundamental reasoning deficits.

title: IndexCache: Accelerating Sparse Attention via Cross-Layer Index Reuse
task: Accelerating sparse attention via cross-layer index reuse
inputs: Long-context LLM prefill and decode sequences
outputs: Attention-weighted token selections with reduced computation
key_results: 1.82× prefill speedup, 1.48× decode speedup; removes 75% of indexer computations with negligible quality loss
comments: Clever exploitation of redundancy across layers; practical gains on production-scale models validate the approach.

title: Video-Based Reward Modeling for Computer-Use Agents
task: Reward modeling for computer-using agent evaluation
inputs: User instruction and execution video (keyframes)
outputs: Task success probability and temporal attribution
key_results: 84.7% accuracy, 87.7% recall; outperforms GPT-4o and Gemini-3 Pro on cross-platform evaluation
comments: Video-based rewards are method-agnostic and scalable; spatiotemporal token pruning elegantly handles high-resolution inputs.

title: ShotVerse: Advancing Cinematic Camera Control for Text-Driven Multi-Shot Video Creation
task: Cinematic camera control for multi-shot video generation
inputs: Text descriptions and optional camera trajectories
outputs: Multi-shot videos with globally aligned camera motions
key_results: Superior cinematic aesthetics; bridges gap between unreliable text control and labor-intensive manual plotting
comments: Data-centric approach with automated calibration pipeline is elegant; ShotVerse-Bench provides needed evaluation foundation.

title: DreamVideo-Omni: Omni-Motion Controlled Multi-Subject Video Customization with Latent Identity Reinforcement Learning
task: Multi-subject identity and omni-motion controlled video synthesis
inputs: Subject appearances, global motion, local dynamics, camera movements
outputs: High-quality multi-subject videos with precise motion control
key_results: Superior identity preservation and motion controllability; latent identity reward learning mitigates degradation
comments: Two-stage training with latent reward feedback is novel; group/role embeddings elegantly disentangle multi-subject scenes.

title: XSkill: Continual Learning from Experience and Skills in Multimodal Agents
task: Continual learning from experience and skills in multimodal agents
inputs: Visual observations and multi-path rollouts
outputs: Visually-grounded experiences and skills for tool use
key_results: Consistent substantial improvements over baselines on five benchmarks; superior zero-shot generalization
comments: Dual-stream knowledge extraction is well-motivated; grounding in visual context makes the approach practical for embodied agents.

title: DVD: Deterministic Video Depth Estimation with Generative Priors
task: Deterministic video depth estimation with generative priors
inputs: Video sequences from pretrained diffusion models
outputs: Dense depth maps with sharp boundaries and temporal coherence
key_results: State-of-the-art zero-shot performance; uses 163× less task-specific data than baselines
comments: Repurposing diffusion timesteps as structural anchors is clever; latent manifold rectification effectively mitigates over-smoothing.

title: WeEdit: A Dataset, Benchmark and Glyph-Guided Framework for Text-centric Image Editing
task: Text-centric image editing with glyph guidance
inputs: Source images and editing instructions
outputs: Edited images with modified, translated, or rearranged text
key_results: Outperforms prior models; 330K training pairs across 15 languages; multilingual support
comments: HTML-based automatic pipeline for data generation is scalable; multi-objective RL aligns competing objectives effectively.

title: Trust Your Critic: Robust Reward Modeling and Reinforcement Learning for Faithful Image Editing and Generation
task: Robust reward modeling for faithful image editing and generation
inputs: Images, instructions, and execution/consistency/adherence metrics
outputs: Reliability scores via Base-and-Bonus reward strategy
key_results: Superior alignment with human judgment; FIRM-Qwen-Edit and FIRM-SD3.5 achieve substantial performance gains
comments: Data curation pipelines for editing vs. generation are well-designed; CME and QMA reward decomposition is principled.

title: One Model, Many Budgets: Elastic Latent Interfaces for Diffusion Transformers
task: Elastic latency-quality trade-offs in diffusion transformers
inputs: Variable-sized images via learnable token sequence
outputs: Generated images with dynamic compute budgets
key_results: 35.3% FID and 39.6% FDD gains on ImageNet-512; generalizes across DiT variants
comments: Minimal architectural modification (two cross-attention layers) enables strong generalization; importance-ordered latents are well-motivated.

title: RubiCap: Rubric-Guided Reinforcement Learning for Dense Image Captioning
task: Rubric-guided reinforcement learning for dense image captioning
inputs: Images and candidate captions
outputs: High-quality diverse captions with structured evaluation
key_results: Highest win rates on CapArena; RubiCap-3B surpasses 7B counterpart; outperforms GPT-4V
comments: LLM rubric writing for decomposed rewards is elegant; structured multi-faceted evaluation beats scalar rewards.

title: GRADE: Benchmarking Discipline-Informed Reasoning in Image Editing
task: Evaluating discipline-informed reasoning in image editing
inputs: Images and discipline-specific editing instructions
outputs: Discipline reasoning, visual consistency, and logical readability scores
key_results: Large performance gaps under implicit, knowledge-intensive settings; models struggle with domain-specific constraints
comments: GRADE fills a gap by testing structured domain knowledge; multi-dimensional evaluation is comprehensive.

title: CREATE: Testing LLMs for Associative Creativity
task: Evaluating models' capacity for creative associative reasoning
model: (LLM reasoning) frontier models including thinking models
inputs: Concept pairs and parametric knowledge
outputs: Paths connecting concepts with specificity and diversity metrics
key_results: Strongest models achieve higher creative utility; thinking models not always most effective despite large token budgets
comments: CREATE provides objective grading for open-ended creativity; result that reasoning scales don't monotonically help is noteworthy.

title: EVATok: Adaptive Length Video Tokenization for Efficient Visual Autoregressive Generation
task: Adaptive length video tokenization for efficient autoregressive generation
inputs: Video frames with varying complexity
outputs: Adaptive token sequences with quality-cost trade-offs
key_results: 24.4% token savings vs. LARP; state-of-the-art class-to-video on UCF-101
comments: Adaptive tokenization motivated by temporal complexity variation is sensible; video semantic encoders improve training.

title: OmniStream: Mastering Perception, Reconstruction and Action in Continuous Streams
task: Unified streaming visual backbone for perception and action
inputs: Diverse visual inputs (images, videos, robotic streams)
outputs: Semantic, spatial, and temporal representations for reasoning and control
key_results: Competitive performance across image/video probing, reconstruction, reasoning, and robotics (unseen at training)
comments: Synergistic multi-task pretraining across 29 datasets enables versatility; frozen backbone generalization is impressive.

title: EndoCoT: Scaling Endogenous Chain-of-Thought Reasoning in Diffusion Models
task: Scaling chain-of-thought reasoning in diffusion models
inputs: Complex spatial/logical task instructions
outputs: Generated solutions with iterative reasoning steps
key_results: 92.1% average accuracy on spatial/logical tasks; 8.3pp improvement over strongest baseline
comments: Iterative thought guidance with terminal grounding is well-motivated; endogenous reasoning beats invariant guidance paradigm.

title: Neural Thickets: Diverse Task Experts Are Dense Around Pretrained Weights
task: Discovering dense task-specific experts around pretrained weights
inputs: Pretrained parameter vector
outputs: Ensemble of specialized model variants via random sampling and voting
key_results: Competitive with PPO and GRPO on large models; simple random sampling + majority voting effective
comments: Finding that large pretrained models have high density of task-experts near initialization is surprising and actionable.

title: The Curse and Blessing of Mean Bias in FP4-Quantized LLM Training
task: Stabilizing FP4 quantized LLM training via mean bias removal
model: (quantized transformer) FP4 (W4A4G4) training with bias conditioning
inputs: Natural language text with rank-one mean bias
outputs: Stable low-precision model activations
key_results: Recovers most stability benefits of SVD-based methods with only reduction and standard quantization kernels
comments: Theoretical insight that rank-one mean bias drives instability is elegant; hardware-efficient solution via mean subtraction.

title: Mobile-GS: Real-time Gaussian Splatting for Mobile Devices
task: Real-time Gaussian splatting for mobile device rendering
inputs: 3D Gaussian parameters and camera viewpoint
outputs: High-quality novel view images on edge devices
key_results: Real-time rendering with compact model size; depth-aware order-independent rendering eliminates sorting bottleneck
comments: Practical mobile deployment of 3DGS addresses real constraint; neural view-dependent enhancement mitigates transparency artifacts.

title: Meta-Reinforcement Learning with Self-Reflection for Agentic Search
task: In-context meta-RL with self-reflection for search
inputs: Search trajectories with explicit self-reflections
outputs: Adapted search strategy across episodes
key_results: 9.2%–19.3% relative improvements over baseline RL; strong generalization on OOD benchmarks
comments: Cross-episode exploration via self-reflection is intuitive; multi-turn RL with turn-level credit assignment well-motivated.

title: Understanding by Reconstruction: Reversing the Software Development Process for LLM Pretraining
task: Synthesizing agentic trajectories for software understanding
inputs: Static software repositories and dependency graphs
outputs: Reconstructed planning, reasoning, and debugging trajectories
key_results: Continuous pretraining on synthetic trajectories improves long-context understanding, coding, and agentic capabilities
comments: Hypothesis that trajectories provide richer supervision than static code is novel; search-based CoT refinement ensures logical rigor.

title: Are Video Reasoning Models Ready to Go Outside?
task: Robustness of video reasoning models to real-world perturbations
model: (video VLM) Qwen2.5/3-VL, InternVL2.5 fine-tuned with ROVA
inputs: Videos with weather, occlusion, and camera motion disturbances
outputs: Reasoning predictions with robustness-aware consistency rewards
key_results: 24%+ relative accuracy improvement; 9%+ reasoning improvement under perturbations; gains transfer to clean benchmarks
comments: PVRBench with realistic perturbations reveals significant robustness gaps; difficulty-aware online training is practical.

title: Training Language Models via Neural Cellular Automata
task: Pre-pre-training LLMs via synthetic neural cellular automata
model: (transformer) LLM pretrained on NCA-generated data then natural language
inputs: Synthetic spatiotemporal NCA sequences and natural language
outputs: Improved language modeling and reasoning capabilities
key_results: 164M NCA tokens improve downstream by 6%; outperforms 1.6B tokens of natural language; 1.6× faster convergence
comments: Surprising result that synthetic non-linguistic data can match/exceed natural language pretraining; opens path for fully synthetic training.

title: Automatic Generation of High-Performance RL Environments
task: Automatic generation of high-performance RL environments
inputs: Environment specifications and reference implementations
outputs: High-performance JAX/Rust implementations with semantic equivalence
key_results: EmuRust 1.5× PPO speedup; PokeJAX 22,320× over TypeScript; TCGJax 6.6× over Python reference
comments: Recipe for agent-assisted translation with hierarchical verification is reusable; contamination control via novel environments.

title: Tiny Aya: Bridging Scale and Multilingual Depth
task: Multilingual language modeling at 3.35B parameters
inputs: Text in 70 languages with region specialization
outputs: High-quality multilingual understanding and generation
key_results: SOTA translation quality and multilingual understanding at 3.35B params; region-specialized variants provided
comments: Efficiency-centered scaling path with balanced language performance is pragmatic; region specialization addresses underserved languages.

title: FireRedASR2S: A State-of-the-Art Industrial-Grade All-in-One Automatic Speech Recognition System
task: Industrial-grade all-in-one automatic speech recognition system
inputs: Speech audio for ASR, VAD, LID, and punctuation
outputs: Transcriptions, voice activity, language identification, and punctuation
key_results: FireRedASR2: 2.89% CER Mandarin, 11.55% dialects; FireRedVAD: 97.57% F1; FireRedLID: 97.18% accuracy on FLEURS-82
comments: Unified pipeline with lightweight modules (VAD 0.6M params) is practical; comprehensive multilingual/dialect support valuable.

title: Geometric Autoencoder for Diffusion Models
task: Principled latent design for diffusion models via vision priors
model: (autoencoder for diffusion) GAE with VFM guidance
inputs: High-resolution images with vision foundation model supervision
outputs: Optimized latent representations for diffusion learning
key_results: gFID 1.82 at 80 epochs, 1.31 at 800 epochs; balances compression, semantic depth, and reconstruction
comments: Principled framework using VFM alignment and latent normalization (not KL) is elegant; strong empirical results validate design.

title: Coarse-Guided Visual Generation via Weighted h-Transform Sampling
task: Coarse-guided visual generation via weighted h-transform
inputs: Degraded/low-fidelity coarse references
outputs: High-fidelity fine visual samples
key_results: Effective across diverse image and video generation tasks; handles unknown forward operators
comments: Noise-level-aware weight schedule elegantly balances guidance adherence and quality; training-free approach is practical.

title: DIVE: Scaling Diversity in Agentic Task Synthesis for Generalizable Tool Use
task: Scaling diversity in agentic task synthesis for tool use
model: (LLM + RL) DIVE with reverse-derived task synthesis
inputs: Real-world tools and diverse toolset combinations
outputs: Executable, verifiable agentic tasks with rich tool-use patterns
key_results: +22pp improvement on OOD benchmarks; +68pp over strongest 8B baseline; diversity scaling outperforms quantity scaling
comments: Evidence-driven recipe inverting synthesis order is clever; controlled scaling axes (tool-pool, per-task variety) are principled.

title: Accent Vector: Controllable Accent Manipulation for Multilingual TTS Without Accented Data
task: Controllable accent manipulation in multilingual TTS
model: (TTS) Accent Vector via task vectors from cross-lingual fine-tuning
inputs: Native speech of different language and target text
outputs: Speech with controlled accent strength and composition
key_results: Fine-grained accent control without accented training data; generalizes beyond English to multiple languages
comments: Task vector approach for accent capture is creative; enables accent control via simple scaling/interpolation.

title: Examining Reasoning LLMs-as-Judges in Non-Verifiable LLM Post-Training
task: Examining reasoning LLM judges in non-verifiable domains
model: (LLM judge) reasoning vs. non-reasoning judges with RL training
inputs: Gold-standard preference annotations for policy training
outputs: Policies trained with different judge types; preference scores
key_results: Reasoning judges avoid reward hacking; policies achieve strong performance but learn adversarial outputs
comments: Finding that reasoning judges still lead to adversarial behavior is important caveat; highlights limits of judges alone.

title: Multi-Task Reinforcement Learning for Enhanced Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge
task: Multi-task RL for improved MLLM-as-a-Judge consistency
inputs: Visual content and evaluation tasks across multiple domains
outputs: Judgment predictions with improved consistency and human correlation
key_results: Outperforms strong baselines in judgment consistency; robust generalization on OOD tasks
comments: Multi-task RL for judge generalization is well-motivated; transfer to clean benchmarks validates approach.

title: PACED: Distillation at the Frontier of Student Competence
task: Distillation at the frontier of student model competence
model: (transformer distillation) Paced with Beta-weighted pass-rate scheduling
inputs: Student model on diverse problem difficulties
outputs: Improved student model focused on zone of proximal development
key_results: Significant gains with low forgetting on forward/reverse KL; two-stage synergy (forward→reverse) strongest
comments: Theoretical proof of SNR-optimal Beta kernel is elegant; focus on proximal zone is intuitive and empirically effective.

title: Dr. SHAP-AV: Decoding Relative Modality Contributions via Shapley Attribution in Audio-Visual Speech Recognition
task: Analyzing modality contributions in audio-visual speech recognition
inputs: Audio-visual speech with varying SNR levels
outputs: SHAP-based modality contribution metrics (Global, Generative, Temporal)
key_results: Models exhibit audio bias even under severe noise; modality balance evolves during decoding; SNR dominant factor
comments: Shapley value analysis for multimodal systems is principled; reveals systematic audio bias motivating future work.

title: SoundWeaver: Semantic Warm-Starting for Text-to-Audio Diffusion Serving
task: Accelerating text-to-audio diffusion via semantic cache warm-starting
model: (diffusion serving system) SoundWeaver with reference selection
inputs: Text prompts and cached audio references
outputs: Generated audio with reduced latency
key_results: 1.8–3.0× latency reduction with ~1K cache entries; preserves or improves perceptual quality
comments: Training-free, model-agnostic approach is practical; quality-aware cache eviction maintains utility.

title: EmbTracker: Traceable Black-box Watermarking for Federated Language Models
task: Traceable black-box watermarking for federated language models
inputs: Federated model instances distributed to clients
outputs: Watermark verification and attribution to specific client
key_results: Near 100% verification rates; robust to fine-tuning, pruning, quantization; 1–2% performance impact
comments: Server-side, black-box traceability without client cooperation is practical; addresses critical FedLM vulnerability.

title: Simple Recipe Works: Vision-Language-Action Models are Natural Continual Learners with Reinforcement Learning
task: Continual RL for vision-language-action models
model: (VLA + RL) Sequential fine-tuning with LoRA on VLAs
inputs: Multi-task lifelong RL benchmarks
outputs: Continually adapted VLA policy with minimal forgetting
key_results: Simple Seq. FT + LoRA outperforms complex CRL methods; high plasticity with little forgetting; strong zero-shot generalization
comments: Surprising finding that naive sequential fine-tuning works well for large VLAs; synergy of pretraining, LoRA, and on-policy RL.

title: Attention Sinks Are Provably Necessary in Softmax Transformers: Evidence from Trigger-Conditional Tasks
task: Understanding attention sinks in softmax transformers
model: (transformer theory) softmax self-attention with trigger-conditional tasks
inputs: Sequences with designated trigger tokens
outputs: Attention distributions and sink probability analysis
key_results: Proves attention sinks are necessary in softmax models for trigger-conditional behavior; ReLU attention eliminates sinks
comments: Theoretical proof of normalization-driven sink necessity is insightful; formalization of familiar intuition about probability simplices.

title: Neural Field Thermal Tomography: A Differentiable Physics Framework for Non-Destructive Evaluation
task: 3D reconstruction of material properties via thermal measurements
model: (neural field + differentiable solver) NeFTY for inverse problems
inputs: Transient surface temperature measurements
outputs: 3D diffusivity field reconstruction with subsurface defect localization
key_results: Significantly improves subsurface defect localization over baselines; hard constraint enforcement via differentiable physics
comments: Discretize-then-optimize paradigm mitigates spectral bias; practical for non-destructive evaluation applications.

title: WaDi: Weight Direction-aware Distillation for One-step Image Synthesis
task: One-step image synthesis via efficient diffusion distillation
inputs: Multi-step diffusion teacher model weights and image-text pairs
outputs: One-step image generator with distilled weights
key_results: State-of-the-art FID on COCO 2014/2017 using ~10% of teacher parameters; strong generalization to controllable generation and high-resolution synthesis
comments: Weight direction analysis provides interpretable insight into distillation, though computational cost of the direction-aware training itself is not thoroughly discussed.

title: TeamHOI: Learning a Unified Policy for Cooperative Human-Object Interactions with Any Team Size
task: Learning unified cooperative human-object interaction policy
model: (humanoid control) TeamHOI with Transformer-based decentralized policy
inputs: Local observations and teammate tokens for variable team sizes
outputs: Physically plausible cooperative behaviors for carrying tasks
key_results: High success rates on carrying tasks; single policy handles 2–8 agents; formation reward ensures stability
comments: Masked Adversarial Motion Prior cleverly uses single-human motions for multi-agent training; scalability to variable team sizes impressive.

title: NerVE: Nonlinear Eigenspectrum Dynamics in LLM Feed-Forward Networks
task: Understanding nonlinear eigenspectrum dynamics in LLM FFNs
inputs: FFN activations across layers and training steps
outputs: Spectral entropy, participation ratio, and eigenvalue metrics
key_results: FFN nonlinearities reinject variance; optimizer geometry modulates variance reinjection; signatures correlate with generalization
comments: Lightweight eigenspectrum tracking reveals fundamental FFN dynamics; insights actionable for architectural and optimizer design.

title: SurvHTE-Bench: A Benchmark for Heterogeneous Treatment Effect Estimation in Survival Analysis
task: Benchmarking heterogeneous treatment effect estimation with censored outcomes
model: (survival analysis methods) Causal Survival Forests, meta-learners, imputation
inputs: Right-censored survival data with treatments and covariates
outputs: HTE estimates evaluated under diverse causal assumptions
key_results: First comprehensive benchmark across synthetic, semi-synthetic, real-world data; reveals performance gaps under assumption violations
comments: SurvHTE-Bench fills methodological gap in survival HTE evaluation; modular design enables systematic method comparison.

title: A Mixed Diet Makes DINO An Omnivorous Vision Encoder
task: Learning modality-agnostic feature space across RGB, depth, segmentation
model: (vision encoder) Omnivorous Vision Encoder fine-tuned from DINOv2
inputs: Multi-modal inputs (RGB, depth, segmentation) of same scene
outputs: Cross-modally aligned feature embeddings
key_results: Consistent powerful embeddings across modalities; retains discriminative semantics of frozen teacher
comments: Dual objective (cross-modal alignment + distillation) elegantly balances consistency and semantic preservation.

title: HyPER-GAN: Hybrid Patch-Based Image-to-Image Translation for Real-Time Photorealism Enhancement
task: Real-time photorealism enhancement via lightweight image translation
model: (GAN) HyPER-GAN U-Net generator with hybrid patch training
inputs: Synthetic images needing photorealism enhancement
outputs: Photorealistic images with semantic consistency
key_results: Outperforms SOTA paired image translation in latency, realism, semantic robustness; hybrid training improves visual quality
comments: Lightweight design for real-time inference is practical; hybrid patch strategy from real data improves consistency.

title: 4DEquine: Disentangling Motion and Appearance for 4D Equine Reconstruction from Monocular Video
task: 4D reconstruction of horses from monocular video
model: (implicit representation) 4DEquine with spatio-temporal transformer and Gaussian avatar
inputs: Monocular video of equine motion
outputs: Dynamic pose/shape sequence and animatable 3D Gaussian avatar
key_results: SOTA on APT36K and AiM datasets; disentangles motion and appearance; trains on synthetic data only
comments: Disentanglement into motion and appearance sub-problems is elegant; synthetic datasets (VarenPoser, VarenTex) enable training.

title: Causal Attribution of Coastal Water Clarity Degradation to Nickel Processing Expansion at the Indonesia Morowali Industrial Park, Sulawesi
task: Causal attribution of coastal water degradation to industrial expansion
inputs: Multi-decadal satellite ocean color records and industrial timeline
outputs: Causal impact estimates of nickel processing on water clarity
key_results: Significant deterioration in K_d(490) after IMIP hyper-expansion; satellite-derived land cover corroborates timing
comments: Satellite-based quasi-experimental framework is transferable for coastal impact assessment; demonstrates quantifiable marine cost of industrialization.

title: SparStVR - exploring sparse 3D histology data in virtual reality
task: 3D visualization of sparse histology data in virtual reality
inputs: stacks of labeled masks and color images from serial sectioned histology
outputs: interactive 3D textured models with embedded measurements in VR environment
key_results: SparStVR enables unprecedented interactive discovery of tissue structures and pathology compared to traditional projection-based tools
comments: This is a visualization/software tool rather than a biological systems modeling paper; no quantitative validation metrics are provided in the abstract.

title: Clinical efficacy of Chaipu Decoction combined with Seretide in the treatment of bronchial asthma and its impact on TNF-α and CD4+/CD8+
task: evaluate clinical efficacy of combined herbal and pharmaceutical treatment for asthma
inputs: patients with bronchial asthma receiving Chaipu Decoction plus Seretide or Seretide alone
outputs: clinical efficacy rate, lung function indices (FEV1, FVC, FEV1/FVC, PEF), serum TNF-α, CD4+/CD8+ ratio, ACT scores
key_results: combination treatment achieved 86.7% vs. 66.7% efficacy (p<0.05); greater TNF-α reduction and CD4+/CD8+ ratio increase; no significant adverse reaction increase
comments: This is a clinical trial paper, not a systems biology study; results suggest immunological and functional benefit but lack mechanistic insight into how the herbal decoction modulates immune response.
hypotheses: Chaipu Decoction as adjunct to Seretide improves clinical outcomes in asthma
Combined treatment reduces inflammatory markers (TNF-α) and improves immune balance (CD4+/CD8+)
results: higher total effective rate in combination group (86.7% vs 66.7%)
greater TNF-α reduction and CD4+/CD8+ ratio increase with combination treatment

title: Time-resolved functional genomics using deep learning reveals global hierarchical control of autophagy
task: map genetic network controlling autophagy activation and inactivation during nutrient changes
model: time-resolved high-content imaging with deep learning and latent feature analysis on 5,919 yeast mutants
inputs: nutrient (nitrogen) changes; mutant library profiled by imaging
outputs: categorized mutant phenotypes by nutrient response kinetics; hierarchical regulatory network; retrograde pathway identification
key_results: identified retrograde pathway as pivotal time-varying modulator of autophagy gene expression and central regulator of autophagy inactivation; comprehensive genetic network resource (AutoDRY) with 5,919 mutants
comments: Excellent systems-level approach combining high-throughput phenotyping with network inference; the discovery of retrograde pathway as a time-dependent switch for autophagy inactivation is biologically significant and mechanistically insightful.
hypotheses: autophagy inactivation upon nutrient restoration is hierarchically and multilayered regulated
retrograde pathway plays central role in autophagy inactivation
multiple previously unidentified regulatory pathways control autophagy dynamics
results: retrograde pathway identified as pivotal time-varying modulator tuning core autophagy gene expression
hierarchical and multilayered control structure uncovered; new regulatory pathways discovered

title: Catalytic Proximal Protein Oligomerization as an Anti-Tumor Strategy Targeting WDR5
task: Screen small molecules for WDR5 oligomerization induction and elucidate mechanism
inputs: Small molecule library; WDR5 protein
outputs: Oligomerization inducers; structural mechanism; anti-tumor activity
key_results: WZ-1 identified as selective WDR5 oligomerizer; triggers oligomerization via thio-disulfide exchange; potent anti-tumor activity in cellular assays
comments: Nanopore screening is elegant for kinetic resolution, though mechanism validation required complementary cryo-EM and biochemistry.
hypotheses: Small molecules can catalyze protein oligomerization through proximity-driven disulfide exchange
Disulfide motif within inducer acts as molecular scout
Oligomerization disrupts WDR5 interactions at WIN and WBM sites
results: WZ-1 binds WBM site, forms intramolecular disulfide with Cys248, triggers oligomerization
Disulfide motif essential for oligomerization initiation
WZ-1 disrupts WIN and WBM site interactions, downregulates target genes

title: Acute physiological and pupillary responses during power snatch and clean & jerk training sessions in elite female weightlifters
task: Characterize acute physiological and pupillary responses to weightlifting training
model: (experiment) elite female weightlifters; 75% and 100% max load training
inputs: Training load intensity; body measurements at rest, power snatch, clean & jerk, cool-down phases
outputs: Vital signs; pupil diameter; correlations with performance
key_results: All vital signs significantly different across training phases (p≤0.001); positive correlation between max clean & jerk and systolic BP in 100% max group (r=0.37-0.44)
comments: Pupillary response measurement is interesting biomarker of autonomic state, though causality between measurements and performance remains unclear.
hypotheses: High-intensity weightlifting induces measurable acute autonomic changes
Pupil diameter correlates with training intensity
results: Vital signs differ significantly across training phases
Pupil diameter changes with training load

title: Unsegmented marine annelids as biomechanical models for soft robotics
task: Characterize biomechanics of unsegmented marine annelids for soft robotics inspiration
model: (experiment) Phascolosoma stephensoni; mathematical peristalsis model; magneto-responsive silicone soft robot
inputs: Live sipunculan specimens; micro-CT imaging; tensile testing
outputs: Morphometric parameters; strain thresholds; locomotion speeds; robotic protrusion capability
key_results: Introvert eversion doubles total body length (8.87→18.75 mm); trunk strain capacity ≈90-110% vs introvert ≈60-65%; peristalsis model predicts 0.5-5 mm/s in confined media; soft robot achieves 2.5× initial length protrusion
comments: Excellent integration of morphology, mechanics, and mathematical modeling; soft robot demonstration validates biological insights.
hypotheses: Unsegmented worms employ distinct regional mechanics for locomotion
Mathematical models can reproduce peristaltic propulsion in unsegmented bodies
Magneto-responsive materials can replicate biological eversion mechanisms
results: Trunk and introvert show distinct mechanical properties and functional roles
Model reproduces observed locomotion speeds with agreement to experiments
Soft robot successfully demonstrates stimulus-driven elongation

title: Structure of a pH-sensitive pentameric ligand-gated ion channel from the Sarcoptes scabies mite
task: Resolve pH-sensing mechanism and ivermectin modulation in mite ion channel SsCl
inputs: SsCl protein; different pH conditions; ivermectin
outputs: Structures at closed and desensitized states; pH-sensing residues; ivermectin binding modes
key_results: Desensitized state (pH 9) adopts hourglass conformation; histidine and glutamic acid residues identified as pH sensors; ivermectin enhances open-state at pH 9, enables atypical activation at pH 6.5
comments: Hourglass conformation is unexpected and suggests mechanistic parallels to cation channels; implications for antiparasitic design are promising.
hypotheses: SsCl uses extracellular histidines and glutamic acids for pH-sensing
Desensitized state adopts distinct gating mechanism
Ivermectin modulates channel activity in pH-dependent manner
results: pH 6.5 closed, pH 9 desensitized with hourglass geometry
His and Glu residues impact pH-sensitivity
Ivermectin enhances open-state at pH 9

title: Structural basis and regulation of GSDME pore formation
task: Determine structure and regulatory mechanisms of GSDME pore formation
inputs: Human GSDME protein; caspase-3; lipids; S-palmitoylation
outputs: High-resolution pore structures; activated and regulated states; mechanistic framework
key_results: 27- and 28-fold pore structures at 3.64-3.58 Å; caspase-3 recognizes DMPD tetrapeptide independently of C-terminal domain; S-palmitoylation at Cys180 enhances pore-forming efficiency
comments: Sequential proteolytic licensing and lipid modification represent elegant two-step regulatory mechanism; structural detail guides therapeutic targeting.
hypotheses: GSDME pores adopt conserved architecture with distinct geometry
Caspase-3 activation occurs via direct DMPD motif recognition
S-palmitoylation enhances pore assembly
results: Extended transmembrane β-barrel and compact engagement geometry resolved
DMPD motif independently recognized by caspase-3
Cys180 S-palmitoylation increases pore-forming efficiency

title: Crystallographic data for Pyrococcus furiosus dolichylphosphate mannose synthase suggest that the enzyme could flip its glycolipid product
task: Characterize DPMS catalysis and potential lipid-translocation by transmembrane domain
inputs: Pyrococcus furiosus DPMS; GDP-mannose; dolichylphosphate; crystal complex from reaction mixture
outputs: Active site geometry; donor/acceptor binding states; TM domain conformations; lipid translocation evidence
key_results: TM domain binds Dol-P-Man in unexpected 'upside-down' orientation with mannosylphosphate in polar pocket; TM domain not essential for catalysis but potentially provides moonlighting translocation function
comments: Upside-down product binding is intriguing; moonlighting function hypothesis requires kinetic validation but is structurally compelling.
hypotheses: DPMS TM domain participates in product translocation beyond catalysis
Dol-P-Man can adopt multiple binding orientations in TM domain
results: Dol-P-Man 'upside-down' binding observed in TM polar pocket
TM domain mutants confirm non-essential role in catalysis
TM domain proposed to facilitate translocation

title: Impact of solvent forces and broken symmetry on the assembly of designed proteins at a liquid-solid interface
task: Investigate solvent and symmetry effects on protein nanorod assembly at liquid-solid interface
model: (experiment & theory) high-speed AFM, machine learning, Monte Carlo simulations
inputs: Designed protein nanorods; mica substrates with different symmetries; aqueous electrolytes
outputs: Assembly phases; smectic ordering; effect of hydration structure on assembly patterns
key_results: Smectic phase emerges from crystal symmetry-induced directional bias; hydration structure modulates assembly; Monte Carlo simulations reproduce observed phases
comments: Coupling AFM/ML to physics-based simulations is methodologically sophisticated; highlights overlooked importance of solvent structure in protein design.
hypotheses: Substrate symmetry imprints directional bias on protein assembly via hydration structure
Smectic ordering is thermodynamically accessible when symmetry-induced bias present
results: Observed smectic phase reproduced by simulations when symmetry bias included
Hydration structure directionality shown to guide assembly

title: Deciphering the groove-binding mode of dolutegravir with salmon sperm DNA through spectroscopic and molecular modelling approaches
task: Characterize DNA-binding mode of dolutegravir and assess genotoxic potential
model: (experiment & theory) UV-Vis spectroscopy, fluorescence, viscosity, molecular docking
inputs: Dolutegravir; salmon sperm DNA; temperature variation; ionic strength variation
outputs: Binding constant; binding mode; thermodynamic parameters; in silico docking predictions
key_results: Binding constant Kb=10³ M⁻¹; groove-binding mechanism (non-intercalative); GC-rich region preference; ΔG°=-15.0 to -25.4 kJ/mol; ΔH°=-198.51 kJ/mol; primarily H-bonds and van der Waals
comments: Comprehensive biophysical validation of groove binding; thermodynamic data suggest kinetic stability; genotoxic assessment requires cellular validation.
hypotheses: Dolutegravir binds DNA via groove interaction (non-intercalative)
Binding driven by hydrogen bonds and van der Waals forces, not electrostatics
DGV preferentially targets GC-rich regions
results: Groove binding confirmed by displacement assays and constant viscosity
Electrostatic contribution ruled out by ionic strength experiments
Molecular docking shows GC-rich region preference

title: Label-free mass and size characterization of few-kDa biomolecules by hierarchical vision transformer augmented nanofluidic scattering microscopy
task: Extend label-free nanofluidic scattering microscopy to sub-10 kDa biomolecules
model: (experiment & theory) nanofluidic scattering microscopy; hierarchical vision transformer; Cramér-Rao Lower Bound
inputs: Few-kDa proteins/peptides (e.g., insulin); ultrasmall nanofluidic channels; visible light scattering
outputs: Molecular weight; hydrodynamic radius; single-molecule characterization
key_results: Extended sensitivity from ≈60 kDa to <6 kDa; achievable hydrodynamic radius ≈1.5 nm; approaches theoretical limits with sufficiently long trajectories
comments: Vision transformer dramatically improves SNR; extending to sub-10 kDa opens biological relevance to cytokines/chemokines but requires ultrahigh throughput for statistical power.
hypotheses: Machine learning can extract molecular parameters from weak scattering signals below classical limits
Ultrasmall channels combined with vision transformer enable sub-10 kDa characterization
results: <6 kDa mass and 1.5 nm radius characterization achieved
Performance approaches Cramér-Rao Lower Bound with long trajectories
